scenario.title: Replay, gap, restart, and disposable-context laboratory
preconditions: Deterministic checks use the real Tower resume/runtime-client/materializer paths and the production Gooseweb Worker cursor functions.
The P02 public-contract fake source is the only fault source and no product API is bypassed.
The implementer starts no stack or browser; browser review later follows the P03 procedure under the sole supervisor-owned lease.
IndexedDB is disposable acceleration and every unsafe observed transition is mapped to one correction phase in P06-P10.

expected_goosetower: bounded replay overlap is deduplicated and a missing source cursor emits gap plus snapshot fallback
expected_worker_store: socket loss is honest and a stale or unavailable socket rejects commands without phantom success
expected_visible: exactly one ordered result or a manifest-mapped first divergence

expected_runtime: one authoritative deterministic team message and delivery
expected_goosetower: restart and source-epoch change require authoritative reconstruction
expected_worker_store: a fresh disposable context cannot depend on the prior cursor cache
expected_visible: one ordered Team Comms result or an explicitly mapped baseline

required: P02 fixed IDs, source sequences, epochs, overlap, gap, disconnect, and terminal facts
public replay contracts remain the only source authority
real replay, resume, materializer, snapshot fallback, stale-command admission, and gateway publication paths
source and gateway generations remain distinct
production socket ownership and cursor predicates with disposable IndexedDB
unsafe transitions stay mapped and cannot be normalized by fixtures
honest stale/offline state and one visible ordered identity after recovery
fresh contexts reconstruct without old browser cache

golden_goose_acceptance_lessons: epoch and revision authority are explicit
queued stale work cannot overwrite newer authority
fresh contexts reconstruct from server truth
bounded background work cannot outrun selected authority

gooselake.reason: P02 source fixtures simulate only public runtime contracts.

goosetower.reason: Existing replay, materializer, snapshot, and command paths are asserted without product fixes.

gooseweb.reason: The production Worker socket and cursor code is exercised without changing product semantics.

auth_account_state.reason: No authentication or account state changes.

protocol_generated_code.reason: No Protobuf or generated code changes.

assertions: stale or offline state remains visible
risky controls are disabled while stale
composer bottom remains inside the viewport

empty.expectation: Fresh context has no stale cached rows before authoritative reconstruction.

loading.expectation: Reconnect and replay are shown honestly.

stale_gap_replaying.expectation: No later state reduces across a known gap.

rejection_conflict.expectation: Stale commands receive a specific terminal rejection.

terminal_error.expectation: Reconnect failure remains visible and cannot be classified as convergence.

default_fixture_leak_assertions: Default URLs have no fixture query parameter.
No fake entity is bundled into the default or production UI.
The cursor corpus is consumed only by deterministic verification.
fixtures.gating: Source faults use the P02 verification listener; visual fixtures remain DEV plus explicit-query gated.

baseline_detected.description: The seeded live source gap panics in the current source-health transition before safe repair.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Goosetower runtime client total request deadline to SSE cursor continuity
baseline_detected.description: The infinite SSE request inherits a total deadline and reconnects from an older committed cursor.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Goosetower session-detail snapshot kind to Gooseweb decoder/store
baseline_detected.description: Reload reconstruction loses terminal session detail even after live delivery succeeds.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Gooseweb Worker/store to React connection-state projection
baseline_detected.description: Browser and source-health projections disagree after reconnect and fresh-context reconstruction.
A higher gateway sequence with a same-epoch source jump from 4 to 6 is currently accepted instead of requesting repair.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Gooseweb persisted cursor to restarted gateway
baseline_detected.description: The cursor has no gateway epoch, so a restarted Tower sequence below the persisted floor suppresses a new source epoch.

schema_migration.reason: Only verification fixtures and assertions are added.

compatibility.reason: The v1 cursor corpus records current protocol behavior without changing compatibility.

rollback.reason: Revert the additive P04 commit; product state is untouched.

performance.reason: Replay is bounded by existing limits; P05 owns calibrated budgets.

security.reason: Evidence remains redacted and contains no ticket, cookie, credential, or provider content.

data_retention_gc.reason: Fresh ephemeral contexts and tiny source fixtures create no retained product data.

deployment.reason: No browser framework, runner, binary, cache, dependency, CI, or deployment configuration is added.

provenance_reuse.reason: Golden Goose stores and batching tests informed safety assertions; no source was copied.

native_tauri_assumptions_removed.reason: No Golden Goose code was copied or adapted.
